title: Terminate or suspend a contract - Termination for convenience | CanadaBuys

breadcrumbs.title: Claims for progress payments and invoicing
Administrative guidance for implementing contract amendments
Suspension of the work and stop work orders
Termination of contracts with financial security
Public Services and Procurement Canada offices outside Canada

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: Termination for convenience

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: Canada may terminate a contract for convenience in accordance with the termination for convenience provision of the general conditions applicable to the contract.

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: You may terminate a contract for the convenience of Canada if:

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: the funding has been curtailed

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: a government program has been discontinued

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: a contract has been mistakenly awarded to other than the lowest responsive offeror

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: other circumstances make the procurement of the good or service unnecessary

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: Termination for convenience also applies when:

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: the client has requested termination

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: a termination for default cannot be considered because the contractor is not in default

structure_blocks.heading: Termination for convenience
structure_blocks.text: a termination by mutual consent would not be more advantageous to Canada

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: Termination claims officer

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: The Price Support Directorate (PSD), Procurement Business Management Sector (PBMS) was designated to provide termination settlement services including the processing of claims associated with goods and services contracts terminated for convenience by Canada.

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: When a contract will be terminated for convenience in whole or in part, the PSPC buyer should involve the termination claims officer (TCO), PSD , PBMS immediately in the claim settlement process.

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: Refer to the lists below for details on the division of responsibilities between the buyer and the TCO regarding terminations of convenience.

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: The responsibilities of the buyer include:

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: preparing contract status reports

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: issuing stop work orders and notices of termination

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: administering the non-terminated portion of the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: The responsibilities of the TCO include:

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: assessing the contractor's request for any upward adjustment of the contract price for the non-terminated portion of the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: requesting the claim from contractor and forwarding claim forms

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: assisting the contractor with preparation of the claim

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: ensuring the acceptability of the claim

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: determining if an audit is required

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: defining audit requirements and arranging the audit

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: arranging for inventory verification and screening by the client

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: negotiating the final settlement with contractor

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: preparing the settlement and release document

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: forwarding the settlement and release document to the contractor for acceptance

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: obtaining the invoice and signed settlement and release document from the contractor

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: submitting the invoice through the client department

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: distributing the settlement and release document

structure_blocks.heading: Termination claims officer
structure_blocks.text: Joint responsibilities of the buyer and TCO include disposing of surplus inventory with the client.

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: Preparing for contract termination

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: Occasionally, the client will require a contract status report before making a decision to cancel the contract. They will inform PSPC of their intention to reduce or cancel a contract by issuing a "notice of intent to cancel" to you. The client may request all or part of the following contract status information before making a final decision to terminate. This report includes:

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the quantity of stores produced during the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the quantity of stores in production

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the value of raw materials or components acquired by the contractor to carry out the specific contract

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the position with respect to tooling and capital equipment, especially if the contractor had to tool-up to carry out the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the status of subcontracts

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the most economical point to terminate the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: the approximate amount of termination claims, if known

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: You must immediately request the information from the contractor and provide it to the client along with any recommendations.

structure_blocks.heading: Preparing for contract termination
structure_blocks.text: Normally, the client's initial request will be to cancel all or part of the contract, in which case you should immediately issue a stop work order. A stop work order is a safeguard to ensure a halt in the work and remove the possibility of further expense being generated by the contractor. To terminate the contract, a stop work order must be followed by issuing a notice of termination for convenience.

structure_blocks.heading: Notice of termination
structure_blocks.text: Notice of termination

structure_blocks.heading: Notice of termination
structure_blocks.text: It is the client's responsibility to decide at what stage a full or partial termination should take place. You must not issue a notice of termination to the contractor until legal opinion is sought and the client’s formal requisition amendment confirming their decision to terminate has been received. In order to avoid further costs to Canada and hardship to the contractor, a notice of termination must be issued as quickly as possible to finalize the implications of the stop work order.

structure_blocks.heading: Notice of termination
structure_blocks.text: You should ensure that sufficient funds remain in the amended requisition to cover the contract value. You must not adjust the funds when issuing the notice of termination. The contract funds will be adjusted on the settlement and release document.

structure_blocks.heading: Notice of termination
structure_blocks.text: You will prepare the notice of termination or partial termination using the appropriate contract clauses and based on advice from Legal Services, send the notice of termination to the contractor.

structure_blocks.heading: Notice of termination
structure_blocks.text: After the notice of termination or partial termination is issued, you must immediately send a copy of the stop work order and notice of termination to the TCO .

structure_blocks.heading: No claim is involved
structure_blocks.text: No claim is involved

structure_blocks.heading: No claim is involved
structure_blocks.text: If the contractor advises you that a claim will not be submitted following the receipt of a notice of termination, you must remove the funding for the terminated items. Since no claim is made, the TCO is not part of this process.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement negotiation and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: Settlement negotiation and dispute resolution

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement negotiation and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: This section provides information on the process for negotiating a settlement. By following these guidelines, you can manage settlements effectively and ensure all necessary adjustments are made.

structure_blocks.heading: Adjustment of funds
structure_blocks.text: Adjustment of funds

structure_blocks.heading: Adjustment of funds
structure_blocks.text: The TCO will adjust the contract funds on the settlement and release document.

structure_blocks.heading: Adjustment of funds
structure_blocks.text: For the non-terminated portion of a contract, if the contractor requests an upward adjustment to the cost or unit price, the resulting claim for the adjustment should be referred to the TCO for review before reaching an agreement with the contractor.

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: Termination file

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: For fully terminated contracts that are not complex you should send the complete contract file to the TCO . For complex procurements and partial terminations, where a portion of the contract is active, send an electronic copy of the following to the TCO :

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: the contract

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: all amendments

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: specifications

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: pricing details

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: pertinent correspondence

structure_blocks.heading: Termination file
structure_blocks.text: any other information relevant to the termination

structure_blocks.heading: Informing the contractor
structure_blocks.text: Informing the contractor

structure_blocks.heading: Informing the contractor
structure_blocks.text: If a claim is involved, the TCO will send the termination claim forms which includes the Procedures Guide – Processing Settlement Proposals Related to Contracts Terminated for Convenience to the contractor and follow-up to ensure receipt and that the necessary action is being taken to submit a claim.

structure_blocks.heading: Informing the contractor
structure_blocks.text: The contractor must complete all sections of the forms in as much detail as possible. When the forms are complete the signed original is returned to the TCO , who will confirm receipt of the claim with the contractor.

structure_blocks.heading: Informing the contractor
structure_blocks.text: If the contractor has any questions about termination settlement procedures or the presentation of the claim, they may contact the TCO directly.

structure_blocks.heading: Audit of claims
structure_blocks.text: Audit of claims

structure_blocks.heading: Audit of claims
structure_blocks.text: Upon receipt of a claim, the TCO will determine the need for an audit. If required, they will prepare the terms of reference and arrange for its completion. The TCO will also review the cost factors reported by the auditor and reconcile the contractor's claim with the auditor's report. The cost implications of any inventory adjustments should be discussed with the auditor and the contractor.

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Surplus inventories

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: If any inventory is declared surplus by the termination, the TCO should send copies of the termination inventory schedules to the client to obtain disposition instructions. The costs associated with disposition, such as packaging, routing, and shipping, are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim.

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: If there are discrepancies after inventory verification by the inspection authority or the recipient designated by the client, then the settlement offer may be adjusted by the TCO .

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Disposition options include:

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Arranging for verification and shipment of all or part of the inventory to a recipient designated by the client. The costs associated with packaging, routing, shipping, etc., are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Arranging inventory verification (by the TCO with the inspection authority of the client) so that the settlement offer may be adjusted to reflect any inventory discrepancies

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Arranging for the disposal of the residual inventory by GCSurplus

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Before submitting the sales request (for surplus materiel and equipment) form to GCSurplus , the TCO will obtain certification from both the client and the director of the applicable PSPC contracting directorate. This certification will state that:

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: the inventories are reasonable in relation to the requirements of the terminated portion of the relevant contract

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: their use is not required for existing PSPC contracts, due to the nature of the goods, and

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: disposal is recommended

structure_blocks.heading: Surplus inventories
structure_blocks.text: Any proceeds realized from the sale of the surplus inventory are credited (less the GCSurplus commission fee) back to the client, to the Consolidated Revenue Fund or the revolving fund as applicable.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: The TCO is responsible for reviewing the audit report, if one was completed, and for negotiating a settlement offer with the contractor. This offer informs the contractor of the amount of the settlement the TCO is prepared to recommend to the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID) for approval.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, the TCO will prepare the settlement and release form, and submit it to:

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: Legal Services for review

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: the executive director, SPDID for approval and signature

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: the contractor for acceptance and signature

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: The contractor’s written acceptance of the settlement and release document and the final invoice are placed on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file with a copy forwarded to you for your procurement file. A copy is also sent to the client to issue payment.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO will advise the contractor, in writing, of the Alternate Dispute Resolution (ADR) services available through the Office of the Procurement Ombud. See Contract Dispute Resolution for more information.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: The TCO will negotiate a settlement offer with the contractor that includes the settlement amount. If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO can involve the group responsible for business dispute management.

structure_blocks.heading: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
heading_path: Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution
structure_blocks.text: Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, PSPC executives with the delegated authority can approve and sign the settlement and release documents. The TCO will send a copy to the contractor for their signature.

structure_blocks.heading: Payment of the settlement
structure_blocks.text: Payment of the settlement

structure_blocks.heading: Payment of the settlement
structure_blocks.text: When a partial payment or final settlement payment related to a claim on a contract terminated or partially terminated for the convenience of Canada is approved and signed by the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), the TCO will:

structure_blocks.heading: Payment of the settlement
structure_blocks.text: place the original of the completed document on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file and make arrangements to implement the approved settlement payment

structure_blocks.heading: Payment of the settlement
structure_blocks.text: send a copy to the client for payment

structure_blocks.heading: Payment of the settlement
structure_blocks.text: send a copy to the buyer for their procurement file

structure_blocks.heading: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
heading_path: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
structure_blocks.text: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts

structure_blocks.heading: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
heading_path: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
structure_blocks.text: Price Support Directorate (PSD), Procurement Business Management Sector (PBMS) is designated to handle claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation (U.S./CCC) contracts being terminated for the convenience of the U.S. government.

structure_blocks.heading: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
heading_path: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
structure_blocks.text: For terminations involving U.S./CCC contracts, the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), Strategic Policy Sector (SPS), will ensure compliance with the certification and termination settlement functions that are required to conform with the U.S. Department of Defense and Department of National Defence Letter of Agreement (see U.S. Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplements 225.870-6 and 249.7000 ).

structure_blocks.heading: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
heading_path: Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts
structure_blocks.text: The executive director, SPDID will also respond to requests from the U.S. government to arrange audits of U.S. government contracts or subcontracts placed directly with Canadian-based suppliers that are terminated for convenience.

raw_text:
Termination for convenience

Canada may terminate a contract for convenience in accordance with the termination for convenience provision of the general conditions applicable to the contract.

You may terminate a contract for the convenience of Canada if:

the funding has been curtailed

a government program has been discontinued

a contract has been mistakenly awarded to other than the lowest responsive offeror

other circumstances make the procurement of the good or service unnecessary

Termination for convenience also applies when:

the client has requested termination

a termination for default cannot be considered because the contractor is not in default

a termination by mutual consent would not be more advantageous to Canada

Termination claims officer

The Price Support Directorate (PSD), Procurement Business Management Sector (PBMS) was designated to provide termination settlement services including the processing of claims associated with goods and services contracts terminated for convenience by Canada.

When a contract will be terminated for convenience in whole or in part, the PSPC buyer should involve the termination claims officer (TCO), PSD , PBMS immediately in the claim settlement process.

Refer to the lists below for details on the division of responsibilities between the buyer and the TCO regarding terminations of convenience.

The responsibilities of the buyer include:

preparing contract status reports

issuing stop work orders and notices of termination

administering the non-terminated portion of the contract

The responsibilities of the TCO include:

assessing the contractor's request for any upward adjustment of the contract price for the non-terminated portion of the contract

requesting the claim from contractor and forwarding claim forms

assisting the contractor with preparation of the claim

ensuring the acceptability of the claim

determining if an audit is required

defining audit requirements and arranging the audit

arranging for inventory verification and screening by the client

negotiating the final settlement with contractor

preparing the settlement and release document

forwarding the settlement and release document to the contractor for acceptance

obtaining the invoice and signed settlement and release document from the contractor

submitting the invoice through the client department

distributing the settlement and release document

Joint responsibilities of the buyer and TCO include disposing of surplus inventory with the client.

Preparing for contract termination

Occasionally, the client will require a contract status report before making a decision to cancel the contract. They will inform PSPC of their intention to reduce or cancel a contract by issuing a "notice of intent to cancel" to you. The client may request all or part of the following contract status information before making a final decision to terminate. This report includes:

the quantity of stores produced during the contract

the quantity of stores in production

the value of raw materials or components acquired by the contractor to carry out the specific contract

the position with respect to tooling and capital equipment, especially if the contractor had to tool-up to carry out the contract

the status of subcontracts

the most economical point to terminate the contract

the approximate amount of termination claims, if known

You must immediately request the information from the contractor and provide it to the client along with any recommendations.

Normally, the client's initial request will be to cancel all or part of the contract, in which case you should immediately issue a stop work order. A stop work order is a safeguard to ensure a halt in the work and remove the possibility of further expense being generated by the contractor. To terminate the contract, a stop work order must be followed by issuing a notice of termination for convenience.

Notice of termination

It is the client's responsibility to decide at what stage a full or partial termination should take place. You must not issue a notice of termination to the contractor until legal opinion is sought and the client’s formal requisition amendment confirming their decision to terminate has been received. In order to avoid further costs to Canada and hardship to the contractor, a notice of termination must be issued as quickly as possible to finalize the implications of the stop work order.

You should ensure that sufficient funds remain in the amended requisition to cover the contract value. You must not adjust the funds when issuing the notice of termination. The contract funds will be adjusted on the settlement and release document.

You will prepare the notice of termination or partial termination using the appropriate contract clauses and based on advice from Legal Services, send the notice of termination to the contractor.

After the notice of termination or partial termination is issued, you must immediately send a copy of the stop work order and notice of termination to the TCO .

No claim is involved

If the contractor advises you that a claim will not be submitted following the receipt of a notice of termination, you must remove the funding for the terminated items. Since no claim is made, the TCO is not part of this process.

Settlement negotiation and dispute resolution

This section provides information on the process for negotiating a settlement. By following these guidelines, you can manage settlements effectively and ensure all necessary adjustments are made.

Adjustment of funds

The TCO will adjust the contract funds on the settlement and release document.

For the non-terminated portion of a contract, if the contractor requests an upward adjustment to the cost or unit price, the resulting claim for the adjustment should be referred to the TCO for review before reaching an agreement with the contractor.

Termination file

For fully terminated contracts that are not complex you should send the complete contract file to the TCO . For complex procurements and partial terminations, where a portion of the contract is active, send an electronic copy of the following to the TCO :

the contract

all amendments

specifications

pricing details

pertinent correspondence

any other information relevant to the termination

Informing the contractor

If a claim is involved, the TCO will send the termination claim forms which includes the Procedures Guide – Processing Settlement Proposals Related to Contracts Terminated for Convenience to the contractor and follow-up to ensure receipt and that the necessary action is being taken to submit a claim.

The contractor must complete all sections of the forms in as much detail as possible. When the forms are complete the signed original is returned to the TCO , who will confirm receipt of the claim with the contractor.

If the contractor has any questions about termination settlement procedures or the presentation of the claim, they may contact the TCO directly.

Audit of claims

Upon receipt of a claim, the TCO will determine the need for an audit. If required, they will prepare the terms of reference and arrange for its completion. The TCO will also review the cost factors reported by the auditor and reconcile the contractor's claim with the auditor's report. The cost implications of any inventory adjustments should be discussed with the auditor and the contractor.

Surplus inventories

If any inventory is declared surplus by the termination, the TCO should send copies of the termination inventory schedules to the client to obtain disposition instructions. The costs associated with disposition, such as packaging, routing, and shipping, are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim.

If there are discrepancies after inventory verification by the inspection authority or the recipient designated by the client, then the settlement offer may be adjusted by the TCO .

Disposition options include:

Arranging for verification and shipment of all or part of the inventory to a recipient designated by the client. The costs associated with packaging, routing, shipping, etc., are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim

Arranging inventory verification (by the TCO with the inspection authority of the client) so that the settlement offer may be adjusted to reflect any inventory discrepancies

Arranging for the disposal of the residual inventory by GCSurplus

Before submitting the sales request (for surplus materiel and equipment) form to GCSurplus , the TCO will obtain certification from both the client and the director of the applicable PSPC contracting directorate. This certification will state that:

the inventories are reasonable in relation to the requirements of the terminated portion of the relevant contract

their use is not required for existing PSPC contracts, due to the nature of the goods, and

disposal is recommended

Any proceeds realized from the sale of the surplus inventory are credited (less the GCSurplus commission fee) back to the client, to the Consolidated Revenue Fund or the revolving fund as applicable.

Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution

The TCO is responsible for reviewing the audit report, if one was completed, and for negotiating a settlement offer with the contractor. This offer informs the contractor of the amount of the settlement the TCO is prepared to recommend to the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID) for approval.

Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, the TCO will prepare the settlement and release form, and submit it to:

Legal Services for review

the executive director, SPDID for approval and signature

the contractor for acceptance and signature

The contractor’s written acceptance of the settlement and release document and the final invoice are placed on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file with a copy forwarded to you for your procurement file. A copy is also sent to the client to issue payment.

If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO will advise the contractor, in writing, of the Alternate Dispute Resolution (ADR) services available through the Office of the Procurement Ombud. See Contract Dispute Resolution for more information.

The TCO will negotiate a settlement offer with the contractor that includes the settlement amount. If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO can involve the group responsible for business dispute management.

Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, PSPC executives with the delegated authority can approve and sign the settlement and release documents. The TCO will send a copy to the contractor for their signature.

Payment of the settlement

When a partial payment or final settlement payment related to a claim on a contract terminated or partially terminated for the convenience of Canada is approved and signed by the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), the TCO will:

place the original of the completed document on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file and make arrangements to implement the approved settlement payment

send a copy to the client for payment

send a copy to the buyer for their procurement file

Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts

Price Support Directorate (PSD), Procurement Business Management Sector (PBMS) is designated to handle claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation (U.S./CCC) contracts being terminated for the convenience of the U.S. government.

For terminations involving U.S./CCC contracts, the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), Strategic Policy Sector (SPS), will ensure compliance with the certification and termination settlement functions that are required to conform with the U.S. Department of Defense and Department of National Defence Letter of Agreement (see U.S. Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplements 225.870-6 and 249.7000 ).

The executive director, SPDID will also respond to requests from the U.S. government to arrange audits of U.S. government contracts or subcontracts placed directly with Canadian-based suppliers that are terminated for convenience.

Settlement negotiation and dispute resolution

This section provides information on the process for negotiating a settlement. By following these guidelines, you can manage settlements effectively and ensure all necessary adjustments are made.

Adjustment of funds

The TCO will adjust the contract funds on the settlement and release document.

For the non-terminated portion of a contract, if the contractor requests an upward adjustment to the cost or unit price, the resulting claim for the adjustment should be referred to the TCO for review before reaching an agreement with the contractor.

Termination file

For fully terminated contracts that are not complex you should send the complete contract file to the TCO . For complex procurements and partial terminations, where a portion of the contract is active, send an electronic copy of the following to the TCO :

the contract

all amendments

specifications

pricing details

pertinent correspondence

any other information relevant to the termination

Informing the contractor

If a claim is involved, the TCO will send the termination claim forms which includes the Procedures Guide – Processing Settlement Proposals Related to Contracts Terminated for Convenience to the contractor and follow-up to ensure receipt and that the necessary action is being taken to submit a claim.

The contractor must complete all sections of the forms in as much detail as possible. When the forms are complete the signed original is returned to the TCO , who will confirm receipt of the claim with the contractor.

If the contractor has any questions about termination settlement procedures or the presentation of the claim, they may contact the TCO directly.

Audit of claims

Upon receipt of a claim, the TCO will determine the need for an audit. If required, they will prepare the terms of reference and arrange for its completion. The TCO will also review the cost factors reported by the auditor and reconcile the contractor's claim with the auditor's report. The cost implications of any inventory adjustments should be discussed with the auditor and the contractor.

Surplus inventories

If any inventory is declared surplus by the termination, the TCO should send copies of the termination inventory schedules to the client to obtain disposition instructions. The costs associated with disposition, such as packaging, routing, and shipping, are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim.

If there are discrepancies after inventory verification by the inspection authority or the recipient designated by the client, then the settlement offer may be adjusted by the TCO .

Disposition options include:

Arranging for verification and shipment of all or part of the inventory to a recipient designated by the client. The costs associated with packaging, routing, shipping, etc., are a proper post termination charge to be added to the contractor's claim

Arranging inventory verification (by the TCO with the inspection authority of the client) so that the settlement offer may be adjusted to reflect any inventory discrepancies

Arranging for the disposal of the residual inventory by GCSurplus

Before submitting the sales request (for surplus materiel and equipment) form to GCSurplus , the TCO will obtain certification from both the client and the director of the applicable PSPC contracting directorate. This certification will state that:

the inventories are reasonable in relation to the requirements of the terminated portion of the relevant contract

their use is not required for existing PSPC contracts, due to the nature of the goods, and

disposal is recommended

Any proceeds realized from the sale of the surplus inventory are credited (less the GCSurplus commission fee) back to the client, to the Consolidated Revenue Fund or the revolving fund as applicable.

Settlement offer, close-out and dispute resolution

The TCO is responsible for reviewing the audit report, if one was completed, and for negotiating a settlement offer with the contractor. This offer informs the contractor of the amount of the settlement the TCO is prepared to recommend to the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID) for approval.

Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, the TCO will prepare the settlement and release form, and submit it to:

Legal Services for review

the executive director, SPDID for approval and signature

the contractor for acceptance and signature

The contractor’s written acceptance of the settlement and release document and the final invoice are placed on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file with a copy forwarded to you for your procurement file. A copy is also sent to the client to issue payment.

If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO will advise the contractor, in writing, of the Alternate Dispute Resolution (ADR) services available through the Office of the Procurement Ombud. See Contract Dispute Resolution for more information.

The TCO will negotiate a settlement offer with the contractor that includes the settlement amount. If the contractor rejects the proposed settlement, the TCO can involve the group responsible for business dispute management.

Once the contractor accepts the proposed settlement offer, PSPC executives with the delegated authority can approve and sign the settlement and release documents. The TCO will send a copy to the contractor for their signature.

Payment of the settlement

When a partial payment or final settlement payment related to a claim on a contract terminated or partially terminated for the convenience of Canada is approved and signed by the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), the TCO will:

place the original of the completed document on the Price Support Directorate (PSD) file and make arrangements to implement the approved settlement payment

send a copy to the client for payment

send a copy to the buyer for their procurement file

Claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation contracts

Price Support Directorate (PSD), Procurement Business Management Sector (PBMS) is designated to handle claims arising from United States and Canadian Commercial Corporation (U.S./CCC) contracts being terminated for the convenience of the U.S. government.

For terminations involving U.S./CCC contracts, the executive director, Strategic Policy Development and Integration Directorate (SPDID), Strategic Policy Sector (SPS), will ensure compliance with the certification and termination settlement functions that are required to conform with the U.S. Department of Defense and Department of National Defence Letter of Agreement (see U.S. Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplements 225.870-6 and 249.7000 ).

The executive director, SPDID will also respond to requests from the U.S. government to arrange audits of U.S. government contracts or subcontracts placed directly with Canadian-based suppliers that are terminated for convenience.
